an8.77:1.1.pli: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:

an8.77:1.3.pli: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato sāriputtassa paccassosuṁ.

an8.77:2.1.eng: “Reverends, these eight individuals are found in the world.

an8.77:2.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:2.3.eng: First, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:2.4.eng: They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:2.5.pli: Tassa uṭṭhahato, ghaṭato, vāyamato lābhāya lābho nuppajjati.
an8.77:2.5.eng: But material things don’t come to them.

an8.77:2.6.pli: So tena alābhena socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
an8.77:2.6.eng: And so they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion because they don’t get those material things.

an8.77:2.7.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, uṭṭhahati, ghaṭati, vāyamati lābhāya, na ca lābhī, socī ca paridevī ca, cuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:2.7.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. But when they do not acquire material things, they sorrow and lament. They’ve fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:3.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:3.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:3.2.eng: They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:3.3.pli: Tassa uṭṭhahato ghaṭato vāyamato lābhāya lābho uppajjati.
an8.77:3.3.eng: And material things do come to them.

an8.77:3.4.pli: So tena lābhena majjati pamajjati pamādamāpajjati.
an8.77:3.4.eng: And so they become indulgent and fall into negligence regarding those material things.

an8.77:3.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, uṭṭhahati ghaṭati vāyamati lābhāya, lābhī ca, madī ca pamādī ca, cuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:3.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. And when they acquire material things, they become intoxicated and negligent. They’ve fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:4.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:4.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:4.2.pli: So na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya.
an8.77:4.2.eng: They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:4.3.pli: Tassa anuṭṭhahato, aghaṭato, avāyamato lābhāya lābho nuppajjati.
an8.77:4.3.eng: And material things don’t come to them.

an8.77:4.4.pli: So tena alābhena socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
an8.77:4.4.eng: And so they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion because they don’t get those material things.

an8.77:4.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya, na ca lābhī, socī ca paridevī ca, cuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:4.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. But when they do not acquire material things, they sorrow and lament. They’ve fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:5.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:5.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:5.2.pli: So na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya.
an8.77:5.2.eng: They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:5.3.pli: Tassa anuṭṭhahato, aghaṭato, avāyamato lābhāya lābho uppajjati.
an8.77:5.3.eng: But material things do come to them.

an8.77:5.4.pli: So tena lābhena majjati pamajjati pamādamāpajjati.
an8.77:5.4.eng: And so they become indulgent and fall into negligence regarding those material things.

an8.77:5.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, na uṭṭhahati na ghaṭati na vāyamati lābhāya, lābhī ca, madī ca pamādī ca, cuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:5.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. But when they acquire material things, they become intoxicated and negligent. They’ve fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:6.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:6.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:6.2.eng: They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:6.3.pli: Tassa uṭṭhahato, ghaṭato, vāyamato lābhāya, lābho nuppajjati.
an8.77:6.3.eng: But material things don’t come to them.

an8.77:6.4.pli: So tena alābhena na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
an8.77:6.4.eng: But they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion because they don’t get those material things.

an8.77:6.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, uṭṭhahati ghaṭati vāyamati lābhāya, na ca lābhī, na ca socī na ca paridevī, accuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:6.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. But when they do not acquire material things, they don’t sorrow and lament. They haven’t fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:7.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:7.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:7.2.eng: They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:7.3.pli: Tassa uṭṭhahato, ghaṭato, vāyamato lābhāya, lābho uppajjati.
an8.77:7.3.eng: And material things do come to them.

an8.77:7.4.pli: So tena lābhena na majjati na pamajjati na pamādamāpajjati.
an8.77:7.4.eng: But they don’t become indulgent and fall into negligence regarding those material things.

an8.77:7.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, uṭṭhahati, ghaṭati, vāyamati lābhāya, lābhī ca, na ca madī na ca pamādī, accuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:7.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. But when they acquire material things, they don’t become intoxicated and negligent. They haven’t fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:8.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:8.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:8.2.pli: So na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya.
an8.77:8.2.eng: They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:8.3.pli: Tassa anuṭṭhahato, aghaṭato, avāyamato lābhāya, lābho nuppajjati.
an8.77:8.3.eng: And material things don’t come to them.

an8.77:8.4.pli: So tena alābhena na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
an8.77:8.4.eng: But they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion because they don’t get those material things.

an8.77:8.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya, na ca lābhī, na ca socī na ca paridevī, accuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:8.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. And when they do not acquire material things, they don’t sorrow and lament. They haven’t fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:9.1.pli: Idha panāvuso, bhikkhuno pavivittassa viharato nirāyattavuttino icchā uppajjati lābhāya.
an8.77:9.1.eng: Next, when a mendicant stays secluded, living independently, a desire arises for material things.

an8.77:9.2.pli: So na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya.
an8.77:9.2.eng: They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them.

an8.77:9.3.pli: Tassa anuṭṭhahato, aghaṭato, avāyamato lābhāya, lābho uppajjati.
an8.77:9.3.eng: But material things do come to them.

an8.77:9.4.pli: So tena lābhena na majjati na pamajjati na pamādamāpajjati.
an8.77:9.4.eng: But they don’t become indulgent and fall into negligence regarding those material things.

an8.77:9.5.pli: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, ‘bhikkhu iccho viharati lābhāya, na uṭṭhahati, na ghaṭati, na vāyamati lābhāya, lābhī ca, na ca madī na ca pamādī, accuto ca saddhammā’.
an8.77:9.5.eng: This is called a mendicant who lives desiring material things. They don’t try hard, strive, and make an effort to get them. And when they acquire material things, they don’t become intoxicated and negligent. They haven’t fallen from the true teaching.

an8.77:9.6.pli: Ime kho, āvuso, aṭṭha puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.
an8.77:9.6.eng: These eight individuals are found in the world.”

an8.78:1.1.pli: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi …pe…

an8.78:1.2.pli: chahāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, alaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:1.2.eng: “Reverends, a mendicant with six qualities is good enough for themselves and others.

an8.78:1.4.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:1.4.eng: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:1.5.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:1.6.eng: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:1.7.eng: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:1.8.pli: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
an8.78:1.8.eng: They’re a good speaker who enunciates well. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.

an8.78:1.9.pli: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
an8.78:1.9.eng: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:1.10.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, alaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:1.10.eng: A mendicant with these six qualities is good enough for themselves and others.

an8.78:2.1.pli: Pañcahāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, alaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:2.1.eng: A mendicant with five qualities is good enough for themselves and others.

an8.78:2.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:2.3.eng: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:2.4.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:2.5.eng: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:2.6.eng: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:2.7.eng: They’re a good speaker. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.

an8.78:2.8.eng: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:2.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, alaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:2.9.eng: A mendicant with these five qualities is good enough for themselves and others.

an8.78:3.1.pli: Catūhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:3.1.eng: A mendicant with four qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:3.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:3.3.eng: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:3.4.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:3.5.eng: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:3.6.eng: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:3.7.eng: They’re not a good speaker. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.

an8.78:3.8.eng: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:3.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:3.9.eng: A mendicant with these four qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:4.1.pli: Catūhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
an8.78:4.1.eng: A mendicant with four qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.

an8.78:4.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:4.3.eng: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:4.4.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:4.5.pli: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
an8.78:4.5.eng: But they don’t examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:4.6.pli: no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
an8.78:4.6.eng: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:4.7.eng: They’re a good speaker. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.

an8.78:4.8.eng: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:4.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
an8.78:4.9.eng: A mendicant with these four qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.

an8.78:5.1.pli: Tīhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:5.1.eng: A mendicant with three qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:5.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:5.3.eng: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:5.4.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:5.5.eng: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:5.6.eng: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:5.7.eng: They’re not a good speaker. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.

an8.78:5.8.eng: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:5.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:5.9.eng: A mendicant with these three qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:6.1.pli: Tīhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
an8.78:6.1.eng: A mendicant with three qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.

an8.78:6.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:6.3.eng: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:6.4.eng: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:6.5.pli: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
an8.78:6.5.eng: But they don’t examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:6.6.pli: no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
an8.78:6.6.eng: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:6.7.eng: They’re a good speaker. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.

an8.78:6.8.eng: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:6.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
an8.78:6.9.eng: A mendicant with these three qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.

an8.78:7.1.pli: Dvīhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:7.1.eng: A mendicant with two qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:7.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:7.3.eng: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:7.4.pli: no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti;
an8.78:7.4.eng: And they don’t readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:7.5.eng: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:7.6.eng: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:7.7.eng: They’re not a good speaker. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.

an8.78:7.8.eng: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:7.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
an8.78:7.9.eng: A mendicant with these two qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.

an8.78:8.1.pli: Dvīhāvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
an8.78:8.1.eng: A mendicant with two qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.

an8.78:8.3.pli: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
an8.78:8.3.eng: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.

an8.78:8.4.pli: no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti;
an8.78:8.4.eng: And they don’t readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.

an8.78:8.5.pli: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
an8.78:8.5.eng: Nor do they examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.

an8.78:8.6.pli: no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
an8.78:8.6.eng: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.

an8.78:8.7.pli: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
an8.78:8.7.eng: They’re a good speaker who enunciates well. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.

an8.78:8.8.pli: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
an8.78:8.8.eng: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.

an8.78:8.9.pli: Imehi kho, āvuso, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano”ti.
an8.78:8.9.eng: A mendicant with these two qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.”

an8.79:1.1.pli: “Aṭṭhime, bhikkhave, dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattanti.
an8.79:1.1.eng: “These eight things lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.

an8.79:1.3.pli: Kammārāmatā, bhassārāmatā, niddārāmatā, saṅgaṇikārāmatā, indriyesu aguttadvāratā, bhojane amattaññutā, saṁsaggārāmatā, papañcārāmatā—
an8.79:1.3.eng: They relish work, talk, sleep, and company. They don’t guard the sense doors and they eat too much. They relish closeness and proliferation.

an8.79:1.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭha dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattanti.
an8.79:1.4.eng: These eight things lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.

an8.79:2.1.pli: Aṭṭhime, bhikkhave, dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattanti.
an8.79:2.1.eng: These eight things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.

an8.79:2.3.pli: Na kammārāmatā, na bhassārāmatā, na niddārāmatā, na saṅgaṇikārāmatā, indriyesu guttadvāratā, bhojane mattaññutā, asaṁsaggārāmatā, nippapañcārāmatā—
an8.79:2.3.eng: They don’t relish work, talk, sleep and company. They guard the sense doors, and they don’t eat too much. They don’t relish closeness and proliferation.

an8.79:2.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭha dhammā sekhassa bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattantī”ti.
an8.79:2.4.eng: These eight things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant trainee.”

an8.8:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā uttaro mahisavatthusmiṁ viharati saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ.
an8.8:1.1.eng: At one time Venerable Uttara was staying on the Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in the Mahisa region near Dhavajālikā.

an8.8:1.2.pli: Tatra kho āyasmā uttaro bhikkhū āmantesi:

an8.8:1.3.pli: “sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
an8.8:1.3.eng: “Mendicants, it’s good for a mendicant to check their own failings from time to time.

an8.8:1.4.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
an8.8:1.4.eng: It’s good for a mendicant to check the failings of others from time to time.

an8.8:1.5.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
an8.8:1.5.eng: It’s good for a mendicant to check their own successes from time to time.

an8.8:1.6.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ parasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hotī”ti.
an8.8:1.6.eng: It’s good for a mendicant to check the successes of others from time to time.”

an8.8:2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena vessavaṇo mahārājā uttarāya disāya dakkhiṇaṁ disaṁ gacchati kenacideva karaṇīyena.
an8.8:2.1.eng: Now at that time the great king Vessavaṇa was on his way from the north to the south on some business.

an8.8:2.2.pli: Assosi kho vessavaṇo mahārājā āyasmato uttarassa mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desentassa:
an8.8:2.2.eng: He heard Venerable Uttara teaching this to the mendicants on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain.

an8.8:2.3.pli: “sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.

an8.8:2.4.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.

an8.8:2.5.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.

an8.8:2.6.pli: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ parasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hotī”ti.

an8.8:3.1.pli: Atha kho vessavaṇo mahārājā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
an8.8:3.1.eng: Then Vessavaṇa vanished from Saṅkheyyaka Mountain and appeared among the gods of the thirty-three, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm.

an8.8:3.2.pli: Atha kho vessavaṇo mahārājā yena sakko devānamindo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ etadavoca:
an8.8:3.2.eng: Then he went up to Sakka, lord of gods, and said to him:

an8.8:3.3.eng: “Please good fellow, you should know this.

an8.8:3.4.pli: Eso āyasmā uttaro mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
an8.8:3.4.eng: Venerable Uttara is teaching the mendicants on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in this way:

an8.8:3.5.pli: ‘sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
an8.8:3.5.eng: ‘It’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings. …

an8.8:4.1.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ devesu tāvatiṁsesu antarahito mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ āyasmato uttarassa sammukhe pāturahosi.
an8.8:4.1.eng: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, Sakka vanished from the gods of the thirty-three and reappeared on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in front of Venerable Uttara.

an8.8:4.2.pli: Atha kho sakko devānamindo yenāyasmā uttaro tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ uttaraṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho sakko devānamindo āyasmantaṁ uttaraṁ etadavoca:
an8.8:4.2.eng: Then Sakka went up to Venerable Uttara, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:

an8.8:5.1.pli: “Saccaṁ kira, bhante, āyasmā uttaro bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desesi:
an8.8:5.1.eng: “Is it really true, sir, that you teach the mendicants in this way:

an8.8:5.2.pli: ‘sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti,
an8.8:5.2.eng: ‘It’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings …

an8.8:5.7.pli: “Kiṁ panidaṁ, bhante, āyasmato uttarassa sakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, udāhu tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassā”ti?
an8.8:5.7.eng: “Sir, did this teaching come to you from your own inspiration, or was it spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha?”

an8.8:5.8.eng: “Well then, lord of gods, I shall give you a simile.

an8.8:5.9.pli: Upamāya m’idhekacce viññū purisā bhāsitassa atthaṁ ājānanti.
an8.8:5.9.eng: For by means of a simile some sensible people understand the meaning of what is said.

an8.8:6.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, devānaminda, gāmassa vā nigamassa vā avidūre mahādhaññarāsi.
an8.8:6.1.eng: Suppose there was a large heap of grain not far from a town or village.

an8.8:6.2.eng: And a large crowd were to take away grain

an8.8:6.3.eng: with carrying poles, baskets, hip sacks, or their cupped hands.

an8.8:6.4.pli: Yo nu kho, devānaminda, taṁ mahājanakāyaṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ puccheyya:
an8.8:6.4.eng: If someone were to go to that crowd and ask them

an8.8:6.5.pli: ‘kuto imaṁ dhaññaṁ āharathā’ti, kathaṁ byākaramāno nu kho, devānaminda, so mahājanakāyo sammā byākaramāno byākareyyā”ti?
an8.8:6.5.eng: where they got the grain from, how should that crowd rightly reply?”

an8.8:6.6.pli: “‘Amumhā mahādhaññarāsimhā āharāmā’ti kho, bhante, so mahājanakāyo sammā byākaramāno byākareyyā”ti.
an8.8:6.6.eng: “Sir, they should reply that they took it from the large heap of grain.”

an8.8:6.7.pli: “Evamevaṁ kho, devānaminda, yaṁ kiñci subhāsitaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
an8.8:6.7.eng: “In the same way, lord of gods, whatever is well spoken is spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.

an8.8:6.8.pli: Tato upādāyupādāya mayaṁ caññe ca bhaṇāmā”ti.
an8.8:6.8.eng: Both myself and others rely completely on that when we speak.”

an8.8:7.2.eng: How well this was said by Venerable Uttara!

an8.8:7.3.pli: ‘yaṁ kiñci subhāsitaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
an8.8:7.3.eng: ‘Whatever is well spoken is spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.

an8.8:7.4.eng: Both myself and others rely completely on that when we speak.’

an8.8:7.5.pli: Ekamidaṁ, bhante uttara, samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate acirapakkante devadatte.
an8.8:7.5.eng: At one time, Honorable Uttara, the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain, not long after Devadatta had left.

an8.8:7.6.pli: Tatra kho bhagavā devadattaṁ ārabbha bhikkhū āmantesi:
an8.8:7.6.eng: There the Buddha spoke to the mendicants about Devadatta:

an8.8:8.1.pli: ‘Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
an8.8:8.1.eng: ‘Mendicants, it’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings …

an8.8:8.2.pli: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ …pe…

an8.8:8.5.pli: Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
an8.8:8.5.eng: Overcome and overwhelmed by eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.

an8.8:8.7.pli: Lābhena hi, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho;

an8.8:8.14.pli: pāpamittatāya, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
an8.8:8.14.eng: bad friendship, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.

an8.8:8.15.pli: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
an8.8:8.15.eng: Overcome and overwhelmed by these eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.

an8.8:9.1.pli: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
an8.8:9.1.eng: It’s good for a mendicant, whenever they encounter it, to overcome gain …

an8.8:10.1.pli: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
an8.8:10.1.eng: What advantage does a mendicant gain by overcoming these eight things?

an8.8:11.1.pli: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
an8.8:11.1.eng: The distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without overcoming these eight things do not arise when they have overcome them.

an8.8:11.8.pli: uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.

an8.8:11.9.pli: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
an8.8:11.9.eng: This is the advantage that a mendicant gains by overcoming these eight things.

an8.8:12.1.eng: So you should train like this:

an8.8:12.2.eng: “Whenever we encounter it, we will overcome gain …

an8.8:13.1.pli: Ettāvatā, bhante uttara, manussesu catasso parisā—
an8.8:13.1.eng: Honorable Uttara, this exposition of the teaching is not established anywhere in the four assemblies—

an8.8:13.4.pli: Uggaṇhatu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.
an8.8:13.4.eng: Sir, learn this exposition of the teaching!

an8.8:13.5.pli: Pariyāpuṇātu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.
an8.8:13.5.eng: Memorize this exposition of the teaching!

an8.8:13.6.pli: Dhāretu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.
an8.8:13.6.eng: Remember this exposition of the teaching!

an8.8:13.7.eng: Sir, this exposition of the teaching is beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life.”
